model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Virginia is one of the strongest 3-seeds in the bracket, ranking 13th in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +26.49, driven by an elite defense ranked 14th nationally (AdjD 95.7) and a strong offense (AdjO 122.2, 28th nationally), while Wright State's SRS of just 0.12 (152nd nationally) and a defense ranked 155th reflect a 23-11 record built against the 242nd-ranked schedule in the country. The Cavaliers' ACC-tested roster with a 29-4 record vastly outclasses Wright State's Horizon League champions, whose respectable offensive rating of 116.5 was inflated by weak competition and will struggle against Virginia's suffocating defensive system. With 3-seeds historically defeating 14-seeds roughly 85% of the time and this particular matchup featuring a wider quality gap than the typical 3/14 game, Virginia should advance comfortably.

reasoningStep.title: Virginia vs Wright State – Midwest 3/14 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia's elite defense (14th nationally) and massive efficiency margin advantage over Wright State make this one of the more lopsided 3/14 matchups. Wright State's Horizon League championship was earned against weak competition (SOS 242nd), and their 155th-ranked defense will struggle to contain Virginia's top-30 offense.
evidence: Virginia: KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49, AdjO 122.2 (28th), AdjD 95.7 (14th); Torvik #14; Record 29-4; SRS 21.59 (19th); AP #10
Wright State: Record 23-11, Horizon League champs; SRS 0.12 (152nd); SOS -4.44 (242nd); ORtg 116.5 (53rd); DRtg 106.0 (155th)
Virginia's ACC schedule (SOS 63rd) provides dramatically superior battle-testing compared to Wright State's Horizon League slate
3-seeds historically beat 14-seeds ~85% of the time, and this quality gap is wider than the typical 3/14 matchup

(1:29) Myron Medcalf Mar 15, 2026, 06:45 PM ET Close Covers college basketball Joined ESPN.com in 2011 Graduate of Minnesota State University, Mankato Follow on X Multiple Authors Email Print Open Extended Reactions Get your popcorn ready because this NCAA tournament could be one of the best in recent memory. The top seeds are elite programs with the potential to cut down the nets. The underdogs are dangerous, too. The field also has undeniable star power. Top transfers such as Yaxel Lendeborg at Michigan and freshman stars (see: AJ Dybantsa , a candidate for the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA draft) are all in the same tier of teams this year, chasing the same dreams. It's a lot to consider. But we're here to help. Our annual NCAA tournament guide gives you the information you need to know about each team, pertinent information about any current issues that could impact their postseason, key injuries to note -- and, finally, our projections on their ceiling. We're got enough to help the nerds and the newcomers to the NCAA tournament chaos make sense of everything. Here is what you need to know about all 68 teams in the NCAA tournament. modelTrace.content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile the data:

**Virginia (3-seed):**
- Record: 29-4 (some sources say 29-5)
- KenPom: #13, AdjEM +26.49, AdjO 122.2 (28th), AdjD 95.7
- Torvik: #14, AdjOE 121.75 (31st), AdjDE 95.67 (14th)
- SRS: 21.59 (19th), SOS: 9.36 (63rd)
- AP Poll: #10
- Conference: ACC (15-3, 2nd)

**Wright State (14-seed):**
- Record: 23-11 (15-5 Horizon League, 1st)
- Horizon League regular season + tournament champion
- SRS: 0.12 (152nd), SOS: -4.44 (242nd)
- ORtg: 116.5 (53rd), DRtg: 106.0 (155th)
- No KenPom/Torvik data in the system but clearly a mid-major 14-seed

This is a classic 3 vs 14 matchup with an enormous quality gap. Virginia is a top-15 team nationally with elite defense, while Wright State is a Horizon League champion with a weak schedule and middling defensive metrics. The efficiency gap is massive.

The Cavaliers' ACC-tested roster with a 29-4 record and 21.59 SRS vastly outclasses Wright State's Horizon League champions, whose offensive rating of 116.5 is respectable but was inflated by weak competition and will struggle against Virginia's suffocating defensive system. Historically, 3-seeds defeat 14-seeds roughly 85% of the time, and this particular matchup features a wider quality gap than the typical 3/14 game, making Virginia a very comfortable favorite."
